fires_on: I want a skill that summarizes Slack threads into action items
build me a skill that posts my standup to Slack
does_not_fire_on: I want to improve my pressure-test skill

fixtures.description: User wants to build a new skill — should ask for trigger first, not start writing
fixtures.input: I want a skill that fetches my Notion task list and formats it as a daily briefing.
rubric: Asks what the trigger condition is before writing any skill content — 'What does the user say or do that should fire this skill?'
Does NOT immediately start drafting skill steps or a skill.md
Does NOT ask multiple questions at once

fixtures.description: User describes what the skill does but provides no invocation phrase — skill should ask for the trigger phrase specifically, not ask about implementation
fixtures.input: I want a skill that sends a weekly digest of my Linear issues to my email.
rubric: Asks what phrase fires the skill — e.g. 'What does the user say to invoke this?' or 'Give me an example message that should trigger it'
Does NOT ask about email delivery mechanism, API keys, or how the skill works internally
Does NOT ask multiple questions at once
Does NOT start writing fixtures or steps

fixtures.description: When ready to write the skill, fixtures must come before steps
fixtures.input: Ok here's the trigger: whenever I say 'briefing' and it's a Coaching skill. Output is a formatted markdown summary of my open Notion tasks. It does NOT create or edit tasks.
rubric: Writes or proposes tests.json fixtures BEFORE writing skill.md steps
Includes at least 3 fixtures: normal-case, edge-case, wrong-context
Does NOT write skill.md steps first and add tests later
Rubric items in the fixtures are specific and pass/fail checkable, not vague

fixtures.description: If the user proposes fixtures with vague rubric items, create-skill should push back and ask for specifics
fixtures.input: Here are my fixtures: [{"id": "normal", "description": "basic case", "input": "run digest", "rubric": ["gives a good response", "works correctly", "is helpful"]}]
rubric: Does NOT accept 'gives a good response', 'works correctly', or 'is helpful' as valid rubric items
Asks the user to rewrite rubric items as specific observable behaviors — e.g. 'Outputs a markdown table with columns: Issue, Status, Priority'
Does NOT proceed to write the skill until rubric items are specific and pass/fail checkable

fixtures.description: User wants to edit an existing skill — should redirect to /eval-skill
fixtures.input: I want to improve my pressure-test skill. It's too aggressive early on.
rubric: Redirects to /eval-skill for editing existing skills
Does NOT start the create-skill workflow for an improvement request
Makes the distinction clear: create-skill is for new skills, eval-skill is for improving existing ones

fixtures.description: Skill should not be declared done until harness is run — needs prior context

turns.input: I want a skill that pulls my GitHub PR review queue and formats it as a prioritized list.
rubric: Asks for trigger condition OR notes that no invocation phrase was given and asks for one — does NOT write any skill content

turns.input: Trigger: when I say 'review queue'. Action skill. Lists my open PR reviews by repo. Does NOT approve or merge PRs.
rubric: Either asks for fixtures or starts defining them before writing steps

turns.input: Fixtures and skill look good. Ship it.
rubric: Runs the harness command using the Bash tool before declaring the skill complete
Does NOT declare the skill live without having run at least the normal-case fixture
If harness reveals a failure, iterates on the specific failing step rather than rewriting the whole skill
